<title>Permit new button wrong dialog</title>
<file>Admin/UI/Components/Pages/FormTemplate/PermitForms/PermitForms.razor</file>
<start_line>40</start_line>
<end_line>44</end_line>
<category>LOGIC_BUG</category>
<severity>high</severity>
<description>The permit-steps «جدید» button now opens `FormTemplateEditDialog` via `ShowEditFormDialogAsync(0, ParentId)` instead of `FormSelectionEditDialog` with `FormId = ParentId`. That bypasses `FormSelectionEditCommand`, which clones selected base templates under the permit parent; admins can no longer attach existing base forms as permit steps from this screen.</description>
<rationale>The prior handler opened `FormSelectionEditDialog` and saved through `FormSelectionEditCommand`, the documented flow for attaching child forms under a permit parent (`FORMS_SYSTEM.md` §6B). The replacement creates a blank template through `FormTemplateEditCommand`, which is a different operation and does not clone pre-built base forms into the parent’s step list. On `/PermitForms/{ParentId}`, reached from PermitNew’s «فرم ها» action, users lose the primary way to add permit steps from the base library, breaking permit configuration in production Admin UI.</rationale>
</bug>

<title>Files column length mismatch</title>
<file>Admin/Infrastructure/Data/Configuration/Form/FormTemplateConfiguration.cs</file>
<start_line>42</start_line>
<end_line>51</end_line>
<category>LOGIC_BUG</category>
<severity>medium</severity>
<description>`FormTemplate.Files` `HasMaxLength` was raised from 100 to 4000 in EF configuration (Admin and Customer), but this diff includes no migration. The database column remains `nvarchar(100)`, so multi-file JSON payloads can still truncate or fail on save/sync despite the model change.</description>
<rationale>The comment states the limit must fit multi-file payloads because sync fails when truncated. EF’s `HasMaxLength(4000)` only updates the model; SQL Server keeps the existing 100-character column until a migration runs. Saving or syncing templates whose serialized `Files` JSON exceeds 100 characters will still hit database truncation or errors, so the fix does not work in production until the schema is updated.</rationale>
</bug>
